tasks.description: Create Drizzle schema for arena sessions, participants, transcripts, agent memories, and session briefs — the DB foundation for all memory features
steps: Create `packages/db/src/schema/arena.ts` with tables: arenaSessions (id uuid PK defaultRandom, topic text, mode varchar(50), participationMode varchar(50), status varchar(20) default 'waiting', roomName varchar(255), createdBy text FK→user.id, startedAt timestamp, endedAt timestamp, createdAt timestamp defaultNow), arenaSessionParticipants (id uuid PK defaultRandom, sessionId uuid FK→arenaSessions.id CASCADE, agentId uuid FK→agents.id nullable, name varchar(100), type varchar(20), instructions text nullable, color varchar(7), joinedAt timestamp), arenaTranscripts (id uuid PK defaultRandom, sessionId uuid FK→arenaSessions.id CASCADE, speakerName varchar(100), speakerType varchar(20), content text, source varchar(10), timestamp timestamp defaultNow). Use camelCase JS names with snake_case column names matching existing pattern in agents.ts.

Create `packages/db/src/schema/memories.ts` with tables: agentMemories (id uuid PK defaultRandom, agentId uuid FK→agents.id CASCADE, sessionId uuid FK→arenaSessions.id CASCADE, category varchar(30), content text, confidence varchar(10) default 'medium', createdAt timestamp defaultNow, expiresAt timestamp nullable), arenaSessionBriefs (id uuid PK defaultRandom, sessionId uuid FK→arenaSessions.id UNIQUE CASCADE, decisions jsonb.$type<{text:string,confidence:string}[]>() default '[]', actionItems jsonb default '[]', unresolved jsonb default '[]', nextSessionQuestions jsonb.$type<string[]>() default '[]', createdAt timestamp defaultNow). Import arenaSessions from './arena.js' (ESM .js extension required).
Export all new tables and inferred types from `packages/db/src/schema/index.ts` — follow pattern: `export { arenaSessions, arenaSessionParticipants, arenaTranscripts } from './arena.js';` etc.
Add new exports to `packages/db/src/index.ts` barrel — add them to the existing re-export from './schema/index.js'
Run `pnpm --filter=@siestai/db generate --name=add_arena_sessions_and_memories` to generate migration SQL
Run `pnpm db:migrate` to apply migration to dev database

tasks.description: Add shared TypeScript types for arena memory, session briefs, and transcript entries to @siestai/shared
steps: Add to `packages/shared/src/types/arena.ts` (append to existing file): ArenaTranscriptEntry interface (id, sessionId, speakerName, speakerType, content, source: 'livekit'|'websocket', timestamp), MemoryCategory type literal union ('decision'|'position'|'task'|'open_question'|'learning'), AgentMemory interface (id, agentId, sessionId, category: MemoryCategory, content, confidence: 'high'|'medium'|'low', createdAt, expiresAt?), ArenaSessionBrief interface (id, sessionId, decisions: {text:string, confidence:string}[], actionItems: {owner:string, task:string, deadline?:string}[], unresolved: {topic:string, positions:string[]}[], nextSessionQuestions: string[], createdAt)
Add new type exports to `packages/shared/src/index.ts` — append to the existing arena re-export block: `type ArenaTranscriptEntry, type MemoryCategory, type AgentMemory, type ArenaSessionBrief`
Verify re-export works in `apps/web/src/lib/types.ts` (currently does `export * from '@siestai/shared'` or similar)

tasks.description: Migrate ArenaService from in-memory Map to DB persistence — all session CRUD uses Drizzle queries while keeping the same API response shapes
steps: Import db and new schema tables at top of arena.service.ts: `import { db, arenaSessions, arenaSessionParticipants, eq, and } from '@siestai/db';` — follows same pattern as agents.service.ts (direct import, no DI)
Remove `private sessions = new Map<string, ArenaSession>();` field

Rewrite `createSession()` as async: INSERT into arenaSessions, then INSERT participants into arenaSessionParticipants. Map the DB rows back to the existing ArenaSession interface shape for the return value. The `createdBy` field comes from the user session — add userId parameter or get from controller.

Rewrite `getSession()` as async: SELECT from arenaSessions WHERE id, then SELECT from arenaSessionParticipants WHERE sessionId. Reconstruct the ArenaSession object with participants array. Throw NotFoundException if not found.
Rewrite `addExternalParticipant()` as async: INSERT into arenaSessionParticipants, return the ArenaParticipant shape
Rewrite `updateParticipantStatus()` as async: UPDATE arenaSessionParticipants SET status WHERE id = participantId
Rewrite `startSession()` as async: UPDATE arenaSessions SET status='active', startedAt=now(), roomName WHERE id AND status='waiting'

Rewrite `endSession()` as async: UPDATE arenaSessions SET status='ended', endedAt=now() WHERE id AND status='active' RETURNING * — use RETURNING to confirm the transition happened (race condition guard)
Update all call sites in arena.controller.ts to await the now-async methods

Update ArenaGateway call sites — handleConnection and handleClientMessage call getSession(). For the gateway hot path, add a brief in-memory cache (Map with 30s TTL) to avoid DB query on every WS message

tasks.description: Persist transcripts to DB in the transcript POST endpoint and WS message handler — save to arena_transcripts alongside existing broadcast
steps: Add a `saveTranscript(sessionId, speakerName, speakerType, content, source)` method to ArenaService: INSERT into arenaTranscripts. Make it async, fire-and-forget from callers.

In ArenaController.postTranscript() (line 45-59): after broadcastTranscript(), call arenaService.saveTranscript(id, dto.speaker, 'native_agent', dto.text, 'livekit'). Use fire-and-forget (no await) since the endpoint returns 204 immediately.

In ArenaGateway.handleClientMessage() when type='message' (line 196-232): after broadcastToSession(), call arenaService.saveTranscript(info.sessionId, speaker, 'external_agent', msg.text, 'websocket'). This method is sync currently — use `.catch(() => {})` pattern for fire-and-forget.
PostTranscriptDto stays unchanged — {speaker, text, timestamp?} is sufficient

tasks.description: Build the memory extraction service — LLM-powered structured extraction of per-agent memories and session briefs from transcripts
steps: Install @anthropic-ai/sdk in apps/api: `pnpm --filter=@siestai/api add @anthropic-ai/sdk` (MastraService doesn't expose inference methods, so use Anthropic SDK directly for extraction)
Create `apps/api/src/arena/memory-extraction.service.ts` as @Injectable(). Inject ConfigService for ANTHROPIC_API_KEY. Initialize Anthropic client in constructor.

Implement `extractAgentMemories(agentName: string, transcriptText: string): Promise<{category, content, confidence}[]>` — sends structured extraction prompt to claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 asking for JSON array of memories with categories (decision, position, task, open_question). Use system prompt to enforce JSON output.
Implement `extractSessionBrief(transcriptText: string, agentNames: string[]): Promise<{decisions, actionItems, unresolved, nextSessionQuestions}>` — extracts session-level structured output.
Implement `formatTranscriptForExtraction(transcripts: DbTranscriptRow[]): string` — concatenates transcript rows into a readable format: '[timestamp] Speaker (type): content'
Add MemoryExtractionService to ArenaModule providers array

tasks.description: Wire extraction pipeline to session end — trigger async LLM extraction when a session transitions to 'ended', store results in DB
steps: Import MemoryExtractionService and memory schema tables in ArenaService
Add `triggerSessionEndExtraction(sessionId: string): void` method to ArenaService — this is fire-and-forget (returns void, runs async internally with .catch for error logging)

Inside the method: 1) SELECT transcripts from arenaTranscripts WHERE sessionId, 2) SELECT participants from arenaSessionParticipants WHERE sessionId, 3) Format transcript text, 4) For each native_agent participant that has an agentId: call extractAgentMemories(), INSERT results into agentMemories table, 5) Call extractSessionBrief(), INSERT into arenaSessionBriefs table
Call `triggerSessionEndExtraction(sessionId)` at the end of `endSession()` — only if the status transition succeeded (RETURNING confirmed active→ended)
Add error handling: wrap entire extraction in try/catch, log errors with Logger, never throw — extraction failure must not affect session end response

tasks.description: Add API endpoints for querying agent memories and session briefs
steps: Add to ArenaService: `getSessionBrief(sessionId: string)` — SELECT from arenaSessionBriefs WHERE sessionId. Return null if not found (extraction may still be running).
Add to ArenaService: `getAgentMemoriesForSession(sessionId: string)` — SELECT from agentMemories WHERE sessionId, for the session brief page to show per-agent context.

Add to ArenaController: `GET /arena/sessions/:id/brief` — returns session brief + session metadata. If brief is null, return 202 Accepted with {status: 'processing'} to indicate extraction is still running.
Add to AgentsService: `getAgentMemories(agentId: string, limit = 20)` — SELECT from agentMemories WHERE agentId ORDER BY createdAt DESC LIMIT. Import agentMemories table from @siestai/db.
Add to AgentsController: `GET /agents/:id/memories` — returns recent memories for an agent

tasks.description: Inject agent memories into ArenaAgent system prompt at session start — load memories, include in room metadata, render in prompt
steps: In ArenaController.startSession(): before calling livekitService.generateArenaToken(), for each native_agent participant that has an agentId, call agentsService.getAgentMemories(agentId, 10). Format each agent's memories as a compact string: '- [category] content (session date)'. Cap at 500 chars per agent.
Extend the ArenaAgentConfig interface in arena-agent.ts: add optional `memories?: string` field
In LivekitService.generateArenaToken(): update the metadata.agents mapping to include the memories string for each agent — `{ name: p.name, instructions: p.instructions, memories: p.memories }`
ArenaController needs AgentsService injected — add to constructor. ArenaModule needs to import AgentsModule (or AgentsModule needs to export AgentsService).

In ArenaAgent constructor (arena-agent.ts): after the Role section (section[0]) and before Characters section, check if any agent has memories. If so, add a '## Context from Previous Sessions' section with each agent's memory block wrapped: `<memory agent="{name}">{memories}</memory>`
Adjust the MAX_SYSTEM_PROMPT_CHARS budget calculation: memory chars should be included in the overhead calculation so per-agent instruction truncation accounts for memory space
tasks.verify: cd apps/agent && pnpm build && cd ../../apps/api && pnpm build

tasks.description: Add frontend API functions for memory and brief endpoints — using arena-api.ts pattern for arena endpoints, ApiClient for agent endpoints
steps: Add to `apps/web/src/lib/arena-api.ts`: `getArenaSessionBrief(sessionId: string): Promise<ArenaSessionBrief | null>` — GET /arena/sessions/:id/brief. Handle 202 status (still processing) by returning null. Follow existing fetch pattern with credentials: 'include'.
Add to `apps/web/src/lib/api.ts` ApiClient class: `getAgentMemories(agentId: string): Promise<AgentMemory[]>` — GET /agents/:id/memories. Follow existing request() pattern.
Import the new types (AgentMemory, ArenaSessionBrief) from types.ts in both files

tasks.description: Build the Session Brief page — post-session view at /arena/sessions/[id]/brief showing extracted decisions, action items, unresolved topics
steps: Create `apps/web/src/app/(app)/arena/sessions/[id]/brief/page.tsx` as 'use client' component. Use useParams() to get session id.
Fetch session brief via getArenaSessionBrief(sessionId) on mount with useEffect + useState. If null (still processing), show a loading/processing state with a spinner and auto-retry every 3 seconds.

Render structured sections in card layout: Decisions (text + confidence badge), Action Items (task + owner tag: agent name or 'human'), Unresolved Topics (topic + agent positions), Next Session Questions (bulleted list)

Add a 'View Session Brief' link/button to the arena page's ended state. The arena page is at `apps/web/src/app/(app)/arena/page.tsx` — find the ended state render and add a Link to /arena/sessions/{sessionId}/brief.
Style with existing dark theme: use bg-card, border-border, text-foreground. Use cyan accent for confidence badges. Match existing page patterns (padding, max-width, etc.)

tasks.description: Add memory display section to agent detail page — show recent episodic memories so users see what agents remember from past sessions
steps: Create `apps/web/src/components/agents/agent-memories.tsx` — a client component that takes agentId prop, fetches memories via api.getAgentMemories(agentId), displays them grouped by session
Each memory shows: category badge (colored: cyan for decision, green for task, yellow for open_question, purple for position), content text, timestamp, confidence indicator

Add the AgentMemories component as a new collapsible section in the agent detail page `apps/web/src/app/(app)/agents/[id]/page.tsx`. Only render if agent has memories (conditional on non-empty array).
Use existing UI components: Card, Badge from shadcn/ui. Match the styling of other sections on the agent detail page.

tasks.description: E2E verification — verify session brief page renders and agent memories section appears in agent detail
steps: agent-browser open http://localhost:3000/arena && agent-browser wait --load networkidle
agent-browser screenshot --full screenshots/0010-arena-memory/arena-landing.png
Navigate to a seed agent detail page: agent-browser open http://localhost:3000/agents && agent-browser wait --load networkidle
agent-browser snapshot -i && click on first agent to navigate to detail page
agent-browser screenshot --full screenshots/0010-arena-memory/agent-detail-page.png
agent-browser close
